---
title: "Canonical Zeckendorf Normalization and the Minimal Berstel Adder"
subtitle: "Formal language theory and automata"
date: 2026-04-07
categories: [gen2, formal, submitted]
journal: "RAIRO Theoretical Informatics and Applications"
status: "Submitted"
repo: "the-omega-institute/zeckendorf-streaming-normalization-automata-rairo"
theorems: 44
featured: false
description: "The canonical low-to-high Zeckendorf normalization is non-subsequential with exact prefix-destruction index Delta(n)=n. The Berstel transducer is proved minimal with exact state complexity 10 via kern..."
---

## Abstract

The canonical low-to-high Zeckendorf normalization is non-subsequential with exact prefix-destruction index Delta(n)=n. The Berstel transducer is proved minimal with exact state complexity 10 via kernel separation.

**Keywords:** Zeckendorf numeration, transducer theory, subsequential functions, Fibonacci addition, state complexity

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Machine-verified theorems | 44 |
| Target journal | RAIRO Theoretical Informatics and Applications |
| Status | Submitted |
| Scope | Formal language theory and automata |

## Video Explainer

*Video coming soon --- NotebookLM pipeline ready, YouTube upload pending.*

## Links

- [Full Paper (GitHub)](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/zeckendorf-streaming-normalization-automata-rairo)
- [Lean 4 Proofs](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/automath/tree/dev-automation-integration/lean4)
- [Automath Engine](https://github.com/the-omega-institute/automath)
